summary: A brief biographical sketch of Frank B. Hu, detailing his educational background and the origins of his career in nutritional epidemiology. It highlights his early observation of the link between China's rapid economic development in the 1990s and the subsequent rise in chronic cardiometabolic diseases, which drove his commitment to understanding their root causes.

principles.statement: Public health research must focus on understanding the root causes of chronic diseases that emerge alongside rapid economic development.
principles.rationale: Observing the massive increase in obesity, diabetes, and cardiovascular disease in China during its economic boom in the 1990s demonstrated that macroeconomic shifts profoundly impact population health, requiring deep epidemiological investigation.
principles.source_quote: I was fascinated by those changes and felt it was important to understand the root causes.

signature_quotes.text: In the early ‘90s, as China experienced rapid economic development, it also saw a huge increase in the prevalence of chronic diseases, including obesity, diabetes, and cardiovascular disease. I was fascinated by those changes and felt it was important to understand the root causes.
signature_quotes.context: Reflecting on what initially sparked his interest in epidemiology and cardiometabolic diseases during his fellowship in Hong Kong.
